findings.issue: Home-page ALP card still says 'Building' status pill + title 'ALP · Asset Lifecycle' (incomplete) + body 'New-store-opening · 4 PRDs locked · Lovable protos live. Lead: Karan Muley.' — every framing element here is stale relative to the restructured page (MVP live, no 4-PRDs, no Karan Muley).
findings.fix: Replace card body with: title 'ALP · Asset Lifecycle Platform'; pill 'pill-live' with 'Live · MVP'; body 'NSO platform for Reliance Retail · MVP live for GJ-cluster pilot, Apr-2026 · Phase 2 = AI feasibility + 5 integrations.' Drop the Karan Muley DRI line.

findings.issue: IP catalog row reads 'ALP · Asset Lifecycle Planning' (wrong word — should be Platform) + status text '4 PRDs locked Aug to Sep 2025 (Network Planning · Real Estate · Feasibility · Lease)' (now-dropped framing) + Owner 'Karan Muley' (now stripped from page).
findings.fix: Rename to 'ALP · Asset Lifecycle Platform'. Replace status text with 'MVP live · Apr-2026 · GJ-cluster pilot · 7 of 9 Phase 1 Done · Phase 2: AI feasibility + 5 integrations'. Drop Owner cell or set to 'Fynd · Reliance NSO team' (no individual).

findings.issue: 'Drives the funnel cards on the dashboard.' — 'Drives' is on the banned filler-verb list (tone-of-voice §7). Substitute table requires 'produces / increases / raises' with the metric, or just state the outcome.
findings.fix: Rewrite as: 'Powers the AOP funnel cards on the dashboard.' or 'Feeds the AOP funnel cards on the dashboard.'

findings.issue: '5 leads in GJ cluster on the dashboard at launch' is presented as MVP impact. Source (Screenshots PDF) shows 5 leads but they include a 'TEST' lead and one 'Test RE' lead — not all 5 are real. Risk of overclaim.
findings.fix: Soften to 'Live data captured for the GJ-cluster pilot, including TEST leads during UAT' OR drop the bullet entirely. The other three bullets in this card carry the load.

findings.section: §01 What ALP is for Reliance, Problem card
findings.issue: Bullets 'Lead status tracked in Excel · no canonical source / Approvals chased over email' are an inferred problem narrative — PRD does not state these as the today-problem in those words. Reasonable inference (PRD discusses replacing email exchange) but not directly cited.
findings.fix: Either (a) cite PRD §Lead Creation point 3a explicitly: 'Ability to upload documents and add comments — this ensures exchange of emails is removed from existing process'; or (b) reword bullet to mirror the source: 'Document exchange ran over email · ALP replaces with in-app uploads + comments per lead.'

findings.issue: Per tone-of-voice §3 'default-no Sources block' rule, the §08 Sources block should only appear if the page combines 3+ distinct verifiable artefacts that the reader needs to navigate to. ALP qualifies (PRD + project plan + screenshots), so the block is justified — but the in-line eyebrows already cite each source. Mild redundancy.
findings.fix: Acceptable to keep (passes the carve-out with 3 distinct file types). If trimming, drop the block and rely on inline eyebrows. No action required.

findings.issue: All 10 Phase 2 items carry 'Build' pills. Project plan Sheet2 actually marks all of them 'Not Started' (no start dates filled). 'Build' overstates active development.
findings.fix: Replace 'Build' pills with 'Roadmap' or introduce a 'Planned' pill style. Source-honest phrasing: 'Phase 2 plan · sequenced, not yet started.'

findings.section: §03 Lead lifecycle deviates from canonical §03 = Architecture
findings.issue: Spec §9 D7 documents this as a deliberate override. Compliant — no fix needed; logged for record-keeping.
Subhead is 41 words across two sentences (target: <30 for hero per pre-publish checklist). Trims would help scannability for Apex.
findings.fix: Tighten to: 'Asset Lifecycle Platform. The internal NSO system for Reliance Retail. One record per candidate site · lead → site visit → validation → approvals → legal → fit-out → operational hand-off.'

findings.issue: Tailwind CDN dev warning emitted (standard for this register). No fix.

strengths: All 7 on-page section labels run as a contiguous §01 → §07 with no gaps. Spec §3 page-structure matches the rendered page section-by-section.
Every numerical claim in §02 (9 initiatives, 7 Done, 2 In Progress, 1 Phase 2) is directly mappable to a row in ALP project plan Sheet1.
Lead lifecycle §03 (11 statuses, 7 tabs, 7 timeline stages) is verbatim from the PRD's named lists — no embellishment.
Honesty register holds: roadmap-intent §06B explicitly disclaims 'No PRD or named owner today; treat as direction, not commitment.' Apex-grade transparency.
Author/Date/Version metadata stripped from hero and footer per website-section-authoring §4 convention. Footer is single copyright line.
All 5 product screenshots load, render legibly (extracted via pdfimages -j to preserve native resolution), and wrap in js-lightbox with verified click → display:flex behaviour.
Title corrected from 'Asset Lifecycle Planning' to 'Asset Lifecycle Platform' (PRD-correct + user-confirmed).
post_audit_actions: Apply fixes C1, C2, H1 before sharing (3 mechanical edits).
Decide on H2 (5-leads framing) and H3 (Problem-card source attribution) with author judgement.
M2 Phase-2 pill recalibration is a judgement call — 'Build' vs 'Roadmap' vs 'Planned'; align with Phase 2 PM before ship.
